meta.description: Source of truth for all AthleteLab fields, Supabase columns, and rtmlib/MMPose pipeline alignment.

status_legend.implemented: Field is built and functional in AthleteLab UI
status_legend.in_progress: Field is partially built or planned in current sprint
status_legend.not_built: Field is designed but not yet implemented
status_legend.column_exists: Column exists in Supabase with correct type
status_legend.needs_migration: Column is designed but migration not yet run
status_legend.direct_mapping: Field value passed directly to or read directly from rtmlib output
status_legend.indirect_mapping: Field influences rtmlib output processing but does not interact directly
status_legend.llm_only: Field goes to Claude API only, no rtmlib interaction
status_legend.ui_only: Display and navigation only, never touches analysis pipeline
status_legend.not_applicable: Field has no MMPose/rtmlib relationship by design

mmpose_categories.direct: Passed directly to rtmlib as parameter or read directly from rtmlib keypoints/scores output
mmpose_categories.indirect: Controls how rtmlib output is processed — frame windowing, aggregation, filtering
mmpose_categories.llm: Passed to Claude API as context for feedback generation
mmpose_categories.ui: UI display, navigation, or athlete-facing content only

fields.label: Route / Skill Name
fields.description: The athlete-facing name of the skill. Appears in the training feed, results UI, and LLM feedback.
fields.notes: Under 4 words recommended. Sport-specific vocabulary.

fields.label: Icon / Visual Diagram
fields.description: SVG or image URL for the skill icon. Displayed in the node list sidebar and athlete app.
fields.notes: Should be a clear diagram showing the route or movement pattern.

fields.label: Clip Duration — Minimum
fields.rtmlib_reference: Edge Function pre-flight validation — rejects upload before sending to Cloud Run if clip duration is outside bounds
fields.description: Minimum acceptable video duration in seconds. Uploads shorter than this are rejected before reaching the rtmlib service.
fields.notes: Prevents insufficient footage from producing unreliable keypoint data.

fields.label: Clip Duration — Maximum
fields.rtmlib_reference: Edge Function pre-flight validation — prevents full practice session uploads from reaching Cloud Run
fields.description: Maximum acceptable video duration in seconds. Uploads longer than this are rejected before analysis runs.
fields.notes: Set tight enough to reject accidental full-session recordings.

fields.label: Node Status (Draft / Live)
fields.rtmlib_reference: Supabase Database Webhook only fires analysis pipeline when status = 'live'. Draft nodes do not trigger Cloud Run.
fields.description: Controls whether athlete uploads trigger automatic analysis. Draft = no analysis. Live = full pipeline active.
fields.notes: Node cannot be set to Live unless all completeness checks pass.

fields.label: Node Version
fields.rtmlib_reference: Stored on athlete_lab_results alongside analysis output. Allows results to be traced to exact node config that scored them.
fields.description: Auto-incremented integer. Increments on every SAVE when node is Live. Stored on every athlete result row.
fields.notes: Ensures historical results remain accurate even when node config is updated.

fields.label: Video — Descriptive Label
fields.description: Admin-facing and athlete-facing label for this video. Shown in the training feed.
fields.notes: Include athlete name and what the clip demonstrates.

fields.label: Video URL
fields.rtmlib_reference: Passed to Cloud Run rtmlib service as the video source URL. yt-dlp downloads the clip using this URL before frame extraction.
fields.description: YouTube URL for the reference video. Must be publicly accessible or unlisted.
fields.notes: At least one video with timestamps required for node to go Live.

fields.label: Clip Window — Start
fields.rtmlib_reference: Passed to Cloud Run as clip extraction parameter. rtmlib only processes frames within this window. Critical for preventing analysis of irrelevant footage.
fields.description: Start timestamp in seconds for the relevant skill execution within the video.
fields.notes: Without this, rtmlib processes the entire video including huddle, pre-snap, and post-play footage.

fields.label: Clip Window — End
fields.rtmlib_reference: Paired with start_seconds to define the exact frame range sent to rtmlib. Duration = end - start seconds.
fields.description: End timestamp in seconds for the relevant skill execution within the video.
fields.notes: Clip duration must fall within Basics tab clip_duration_min and clip_duration_max.

fields.label: Camera Angle
fields.rtmlib_reference: Used by Edge Function to select correct Reference tab scale calibration for pixel-to-yard conversion on Distance and Velocity metrics.
fields.description: Camera angle this video was filmed from. Options: Sideline, Endzone, Behind QB.
fields.notes: Must match a Reference tab calibration entry for Distance metrics to return accurate yard values.

fields.label: Video Type
fields.description: Classification of video purpose. Educational = athlete learning only. Analysis = pipeline calibration reference. Both = serves both purposes.
fields.notes: Does not affect rtmlib processing. UI and content organization only.

fields.label: Reference Video Flag
fields.description: Marks this video as the elite benchmark shown to athletes alongside their results. One per node.
fields.notes: Only one video per node can have is_reference = true. Enforced in update logic.

fields.label: Skill Overview
fields.description: Athlete-facing description of the skill. Shown at the top of the training feed before the athlete films.
fields.notes: 2-3 sentences max. Written for athletes aged 14-22.

fields.label: Phase Name
fields.rtmlib_reference: Phase names are used to link metric phase_id assignments. The pipeline evaluates each metric only within the frame window of its assigned phase.
fields.description: Name of this movement phase. Must be unique within the node. Syncs automatically to Mechanics and Metrics tabs.
fields.notes: Names propagate by phase_id — renames do not break metric or mechanics links.

fields.label: % of Clip
fields.rtmlib_reference: Directly controls frame window boundaries. Pipeline divides total extracted frames proportionally by these weights. e.g. Break phase at 10% on a 90-frame clip = frames 45-54 evaluated for Break metrics.
fields.description: Percentage of the total clip this phase occupies. All phase weights must sum to exactly 100.
fields.notes: Incorrect weights cause metrics to be evaluated on wrong frames producing meaningless scores.

fields.label: Phase Description
fields.description: Brief description of what happens in this phase. Admin reference and used in Training Status readiness dashboard.

fields.label: Frame Buffer
fields.rtmlib_reference: Instructs the metric calculation layer to include N additional frames on either side of each phase boundary. Catches movements that span phase edges.
fields.description: Number of frames to overlap on phase boundaries. Default 3. Increase to 5-7 for fast explosive movements.
fields.notes: Without frame buffer, a break that starts 1 frame before the Break phase window begins is missed entirely.

fields.label: Segmentation Method
fields.rtmlib_reference: Determines how the Edge Function divides frames before passing to metric calculation layer. Proportional uses proportion_weight percentages. Checkpoint-triggered uses Checkpoints tab body position events.
fields.description: How video frames are divided into phase windows. Proportional divides by % weights. Checkpoint-triggered uses body position events.
fields.notes: Use Proportional for most nodes. Checkpoint-triggered requires Checkpoints tab to be fully configured.

fields.label: Coaching Cues
fields.description: Phase-level coaching instruction passed to Claude API as context for feedback generation. Describes what the athlete should do and why it matters.
fields.notes: Passed to LLM alongside metric results. More specific cues produce more specific feedback.

fields.label: Phase Link
fields.description: Foreign key linking this mechanics section to a specific phase. Ensures mechanics content is associated with the correct phase in LLM context.
fields.notes: Linked by ID not name — phase renames propagate automatically.

fields.label: Metric Name
fields.description: Athlete-facing label for this measurement. Appears in results and Claude feedback.
fields.notes: Under 4 words. Sport-accurate vocabulary.

fields.label: Unit
fields.rtmlib_reference: Must match the calculation type: Angle=degrees, Distance=yards, Velocity=mph, Acceleration=mph/s, Frame Delta=frames. Controls display formatting of rtmlib output values.
fields.description: Unit of measurement displayed alongside calculated values in athlete results.
fields.notes: Mismatch between unit and calculation type produces misleading results display.

fields.label: Description
fields.description: Passed to Claude API alongside metric results. Explains football significance of measurement. More specific = better feedback.
fields.notes: 2-3 sentences. Football significance not technical calculation. Include elite target value and why it matters.

fields.label: Elite Target
fields.rtmlib_reference: Scoring engine compares rtmlib calculated value against this target. Deviation = abs(measured - elite_target). Score derived from deviation vs tolerance range.
fields.description: Benchmark value representing elite-level performance. All athlete scores are calculated as deviation from this number.
fields.notes: Base on actual footage analysis not assumption. Most critical number in the metric card.

fields.label: Tolerance
fields.rtmlib_reference: Scoring engine applies tolerance as acceptable deviation band around elite_target. Values within tolerance score at or near 100. Values outside penalized proportionally.
fields.description: Acceptable deviation from elite target before scoring penalties apply. Accounts for natural movement variance and rtmlib measurement noise.
fields.notes: Angle ±2-5°, Distance ±0.3-0.7 yards, Velocity ±1-2 mph, Frame Delta ±1-2 frames.

fields.label: Weight (%)
fields.rtmlib_reference: Scoring engine multiplies each metric score by weight/100 and sums to produce aggregate Mastery Score. All weights must sum to 100.
fields.description: Percentage contribution to overall Mastery Score. Reflects coaching philosophy on which mechanics matter most.
fields.notes: Min 5%, max 30%. All weights across node must sum to exactly 100.

fields.label: Temporal Window
fields.rtmlib_reference: Passed to metric calculation layer. Controls how many consecutive frames rtmlib output is aggregated across. Velocity min 3, Acceleration min 5, Frame Delta min 10. Window=1 on Velocity returns null.
fields.description: Number of consecutive frames the calculation is applied across. Snapshot metrics use 1. Movement metrics require multiple frames.
fields.notes: Critical: never set to 1 for Velocity, Acceleration, or Frame Delta calculation types.

fields.label: Depends On
fields.rtmlib_reference: Edge Function checks upstream metric status before calculating this metric. If upstream is low-confidence or skipped, this metric is skipped automatically.
fields.description: Optional dependency on another metric. If upstream metric is flagged, this metric is skipped.
fields.notes: Example: YAC Burst depends on Catch Efficiency — acceleration only meaningful if catch confirmed.

fields.label: Keypoint Indices
fields.rtmlib_reference: CRITICAL. These are the exact array indices used to extract coordinates from rtmlib output: keypoints[person][index] = [x,y]. Must match COCO-WholeBody schema in keypointLibrary.json. Wrong indices = wrong body part measured silently.
fields.description: Array of keypoint index numbers from the COCO-WholeBody schema passed to the rtmlib output extraction layer.
fields.notes: Auto-populated from Keypoint Selector UI. Verify against keypointLibrary.json before going Live.

fields.label: Calculation Type
fields.rtmlib_reference: Determines mathematical operation applied to extracted keypoint [x,y] coordinates from rtmlib output. angle=geometric angle at vertex, distance=Euclidean distance, velocity=displacement/time, acceleration=velocity delta/time, frame_delta=frame count between events.
fields.description: Mathematical operation applied to extracted keypoint coordinates. One of: angle, distance, velocity, acceleration, frame_delta.
fields.notes: Must be compatible with keypoint count and temporal window. See calculation type rules in handoff prompt.

fields.label: Bilateral
fields.rtmlib_reference: Auto mode reads rtmlib confidence scores per frame to determine active side. Higher scores[person][left_index] vs scores[person][right_index] determines which bilateral_pair side to use for calculation.
fields.description: Controls whether left-side, right-side, or auto-detected keypoints are used. Auto-detect uses rtmlib confidence scores.
fields.notes: Auto-detect recommended for all route metrics. Routes break in both directions.

fields.label: Confidence Threshold
fields.rtmlib_reference: Read against scores[person][keypoint_index] for each keypoint in this metric. If any keypoint score falls below threshold, metric is flagged as low confidence and excluded from Mastery Score calculation.
fields.description: Minimum rtmlib confidence score for keypoint coordinates to be considered reliable for calculation.
fields.notes: Default 0.70. Never below 0.50. Use 0.75-0.80 for metrics with weight above 15%.

fields.label: Phase Assignment
fields.rtmlib_reference: CRITICAL. Determines which frame window rtmlib output is extracted from for this metric. Metric is ONLY evaluated on frames belonging to this phase window. Wrong phase = metric calculated on wrong movement frames.
fields.description: Links this metric to a specific phase. Pipeline only evaluates this metric within the frame window of the assigned phase.
fields.notes: Linked by phase_id not name. Renames propagate. Deletion triggers orphan warning.

fields.label: Body Group Filter
fields.rtmlib_reference: Determines which rtmlib Solution class is required. body=[0-16] needs Body minimum. feet=[17-22] needs Body_with_feet minimum. hands=[91-132] or face=[23-90] requires Wholebody. Mismatch causes empty keypoint arrays.
fields.description: Which keypoint groups are used in this metric. Controls Solution class requirement validation.
fields.notes: UI filter only — actual keypoints determined by keypoint_indices array.

fields.label: Low Confidence Handling
fields.rtmlib_reference: Applied by scoring engine when any metric's keypoints fall below confidence_threshold. Skip=exclude from aggregate and redistribute weight. Penalize=score at 0. Flag=score normally but mark.
fields.description: Node-level rule for handling metrics whose keypoints fall below confidence threshold.
fields.notes: Skip recommended for most nodes — prevents unreliable data from distorting Mastery Score.

fields.label: Minimum Metrics Threshold
fields.rtmlib_reference: Edge Function checks this before writing results. If percentage of flagged metrics exceeds threshold, entire analysis is rejected and athlete asked to refilm.
fields.description: If more than this % of metrics are low confidence, reject analysis entirely. Prevents meaningless scores from poor footage.
fields.notes: Default 50%. Adjust based on filming environment quality.

fields.label: Score Band Labels
fields.description: Human-readable labels for score ranges. Passed to Claude for feedback framing. e.g. 90-100=Elite, 75-89=Varsity-Ready.

fields.label: Error Name
fields.description: Name of this common mistake. Passed to Claude as a detected or potential error.

fields.label: Auto-Detection Condition
fields.rtmlib_reference: Edge Function evaluates this condition against metric results. e.g. Break Angle > 52° triggers Rounding The Break error. Confirmed detections passed to Claude as observed facts not possibilities.
fields.description: Metric-based trigger condition. When fired, this error is confirmed and passed to Claude as an observed error.
fields.notes: Format: [metric_name] [operator] [value]. e.g. 'Break Angle > 52'

fields.label: Reference Camera Angle
fields.rtmlib_reference: Edge Function matches video camera_angle to reference calibration. Calibration provides pixels_per_yard conversion factor applied to all Distance and Velocity metric outputs from rtmlib.
fields.description: Camera angle this calibration object applies to. Must match camera_angle values used in Videos tab.
fields.notes: Not required for Wholebody3d nodes — 3D coordinates returned in meters.

fields.label: Known Size (yards)
fields.rtmlib_reference: Used to calculate pixels_per_yard = pixel_measurement / known_size_yards. This conversion factor is applied to all Distance metric outputs from rtmlib to convert pixel distances to yards.
fields.description: Real-world size in yards of the reference object in the frame. Used to calibrate pixel-to-yard conversion.
fields.notes: Without this, Distance metrics return pixel values not yards regardless of unit field setting.

fields.label: Minimum FPS
fields.rtmlib_reference: FPS affects Velocity and Acceleration calculation accuracy. displacement_per_frame / (1/fps) = velocity. Lower FPS = larger frame displacement per unit time = less precise velocity measurements.
fields.description: Minimum frames per second required for accurate metric calculations. Below this threshold Velocity and Acceleration metrics lose precision.
fields.notes: 30fps minimum. 60fps preferred for metrics involving rapid movements like break angle.

fields.label: Minimum Resolution
fields.rtmlib_reference: rtmlib model input resolution is 256x192 or 384x288. Source video resolution affects keypoint detection accuracy — lower resolution source = less detail for the model to extract keypoints from.
fields.description: Minimum source video resolution. Below this threshold keypoint detection accuracy degrades.
fields.notes: 720p minimum for reliable keypoint detection. 1080p recommended.

fields.label: LLM Prompt Template
fields.description: Template passed to Claude API with all metric results injected as variables. Controls tone, structure, and content of athlete-facing feedback.
fields.notes: Available variables: {{mastery_score}}, {{phase_scores}}, {{metric_results}}, {{confidence_flags}}, {{detected_errors}}, {{athlete_name}}, {{node_name}}

fields.label: Solution Class
fields.rtmlib_reference: CRITICAL. Determines which rtmlib Solution class is instantiated on Cloud Run: Body, Body_with_feet, Wholebody, or Wholebody3d. Must support all keypoint indices used in node metrics. Mismatch returns empty arrays for unsupported indices.
fields.description: rtmlib Solution class to use for this node. Must support all keypoint groups used in metric keypoint mappings.
fields.notes: Body supports 0-16. Body_with_feet supports 0-22. Wholebody supports 0-132 (2D). Wholebody3d supports 0-132 (3D in meters).

fields.label: Performance Mode
fields.rtmlib_reference: Passed directly to rtmlib PoseTracker mode parameter. performance=largest most accurate model. balanced=recommended default. lightweight=fastest lowest accuracy. Controls which ONNX model weights are loaded.
fields.description: rtmlib inference speed/accuracy tradeoff. Performance=highest accuracy. Balanced=recommended. Lightweight=fastest.
fields.notes: Use balanced for most nodes. Use performance only for nodes requiring maximum keypoint accuracy on complex movements.

fields.label: Detection Frequency
fields.rtmlib_reference: Passed directly to rtmlib PoseTracker det_frequency parameter. Runs full YOLOX person detection every N frames, tracks between detections. Lower = more accurate person tracking. Higher = faster processing.
fields.description: How often rtmlib runs full person detection vs tracking. Lower values = more accurate, slower. Higher = faster, may drift.
fields.notes: Default 7 from rtmlib team demos. For short clips (under 5 seconds) use 3-5.

fields.label: Tracking
fields.rtmlib_reference: Passed to rtmlib PoseTracker tracking parameter. True=IoU-based tracking between detections (2D models). False=re-detect every frame (required for Wholebody3d — 3D tracking is unreliable with IoU).
fields.description: Whether rtmlib tracks person identity between detection frames. Auto-set: True for 2D models, False for Wholebody3d.
fields.notes: Wholebody3d nodes must have tracking=False. Set automatically by solution class selection.
